# UC-22.15.25 · Clock Skew on OT Hosts vs NTP Stratum

## Description

Surfaces OT servers and RTUs with large `_indextime` vs `_time` deltas undermining trustworthy audit timestamps.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_infra. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Enforce PTP/NTP on all IEDs; (2) fix dual-homed syslog paths; (3) alert >120s; (4) document stratum hierarchy per site. Indexes required: `index=ot_infra`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Universal Forwarder time debug or vendor syslog.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_infra` with `_time` and `orig_time` if extracted.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enforce PTP/NTP on all IEDs; (2) fix dual-homed syslog paths; (3) alert >120s; (4) document stratum hierarchy per site.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_infra earliest=-24h
| eval skew=abs(_indextime-_time)
| where skew>120
| stats max(skew) as max_skew, perc95(skew) as p95_skew by host, sourcetype
| sort - p95_skew
```

Understanding this SPL

**Clock Skew on OT Hosts vs NTP Stratum (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 2.11)** — Surfaces OT servers and RTUs with large `_indextime` vs `_time` deltas undermining trustworthy audit timestamps.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_infra` with `_time` and `orig_time` if extracted. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Universal Forwarder time debug or vendor syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_infra.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_infra, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **skew** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where skew>120` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, sourcetype** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT engineering record and session recording the control owner would use in an investigation. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Single value (worst skew), Bar by site.

## SPL

```spl
index=ot_infra earliest=-24h
| eval skew=abs(_indextime-_time)
| where skew>120
| stats max(skew) as max_skew, perc95(skew) as p95_skew by host, sourcetype
| sort - p95_skew
```

## Visualization

Table, Single value (worst skew), Bar by site.
